body:
MAYOR'S TASK FORCE ON BURNABY ECONOMIC GROWTH AND 
RESILIENCE 
M I N U T E S 
 
Thursday, March 5, 2026, 5:00 pm 
Christine Sinclair Community Centre 
3713 Kensington Avenue 
Burnaby, BC V5B 0A7 
 
PRESENT: 
Mayor Mike Hurley, Chair 
Councillor James Wang, Vice Chair, In the Chair  
Councillor Richard T. Lee, Member 
Councillor Maita Santiago, Member 
David Carley, SustainTech Solutions 
Tom Dielschneider, Svante Technologies Inc. 
Carl Funk, Beedie 
Ron Hyrnuik, Bridge Studios 
Arjun Kadaleevanam, Innovation and Economic Dev. Professional 
Isabel Kolic, Heights Merchants Association 
Jennifer Scott, Vancouver Whitecaps 
Dr. Vidya Vankayala, British Columbia Institute of Technology 
Angie Whitfield, Burnaby Board of Trade 
CONSULTANTS: Amanda Fung, Facilitator 
Jamie Vann Struth, Facilitator  
ABSENT: 
Brandon Atherley, Royal Bank of Canada 
John McCarthy, McCarthy Properties 
STAFF: 
Juli Halliwell, General Manager Corporate Services / Corporate Officer 
Bob Klimek, Deputy General Manager Finance 
Ed Kozak, General Manager Planning & Development 
Karin Hung, Director Strategic Initiatives 
Rajvir Rao, Director Intergovernmental Relations & Public Affairs 
Charlene Liew, Planner 3 
Denise Chak, Administrative Officer 1 

Mayor’s Task Force on Burnaby Economic Growth and Resilience Meeting    
Minutes March 5, 2026 
 
 
2 
1. 
CALL TO ORDER 
Councillor Wang, Vice Chair, called the Mayor’s Task Force on Burnaby Economic 
Growth and Resilience (the Task Force) meeting to order at 5:32 pm. 
  
2. 
TERRITORIAL ACKNOWLEDGEMENT 
Councillor Wang, Vice Chair, respectfully acknowledged that the City of Burnaby is 
located on the unceded territories of the xʷməθkʷəy̓ əm (Musqueam), Sḵwx̱ wú7mesh 
(Squamish), səlilwətaɬ (Tsleil-Waututh), and kʷikʷəƛ̓ əm (Kwikwetlem) Peoples. Each 
Nation has distinct histories and distinct traditional territories that fully or partially 
encompass the city. The City encourages you to learn more about the Host Nations 
whose ancestors have occupied and used these lands, including parts of present-day 
Burnaby, for thousands of years. 
 
3. 
ADOPTION OF AGENDA 
3.1 
Mayor's Task Force on Burnaby Economic Growth and Resilience Agenda 
for March 5, 2026 
 
 
MOVED AND SECONDED 
THAT the agenda for the Mayor’s Task Force on Burnaby Economic Growth and 
Resilience meeting of March 5, 2026 be adopted, as presented. 
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY 
 
4. 
ADOPTION OF MINUTES 
4.1 
Minutes of the Mayor's Task Force on Economic Growth and Resilience 
Meeting held on February 19, 2026 
MOVED AND SECONDED 
THAT the minutes of the Mayor's Task Force on Burnaby Economic Growth and 
Resilience meeting held on February 19, 2026, be now adopted. 
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY 
 
5. 
WORKSHOP #5 - INCLUSIVE ECONOMICS 
5.1 
Chair's Address 
Councillor Wang, Vice Chair, provided the following opening remarks: 

Mayor’s Task Force on Burnaby Economic Growth and Resilience Meeting    
Minutes March 5, 2026 
 
 
3 
"Over the last several workshops, we’ve been building a shared understanding of 
Burnaby’s economy—our strengths and assets, the external forces we face, and 
what it means to be resilient and future-ready. Tonight’s conversation continues 
to build on that work. 
As we look ahead, an important question for Burnaby is not just how we grow, 
but who benefits from that growth and how our economic development choices 
must reflect the values of our city. Burnaby is one of the most diverse 
communities in Canada. That diversity is a real strength, but it may also 
challenge us to think differently about economic development. 
Inclusive economics asks us to consider whether our policies, programs, and 
investments are creating opportunity across the city—or whether barriers still 
exist for certain people, businesses, or communities. This isn’t about lowering 
ambition. It’s about making sure growth is broad-based, sustainable, and aligned 
with Burnaby’s long-term priorities. 
Tonight, we’ll explore how diversity and inclusion may influence how the City 
should engage in economic development—from which sectors we support, to 
how we engage small businesses, to how we attract investment and talent. This 
conversation will help shape the strategic priorities we intend to articulate by the 
end of this workshop. I again encourage you to approach the discussion with 
openness and a practical, solutions-focused mindset. 
With that, I’ll turn it over to the facilitation team to guide us through the session." 
 
5.2 
Recap of Previous Workshop 
A. Fung, Facilitator, provided a recap of Workshop #4. 
 
5.3 
Icebreaker 
A. Fung, Facilitator, led an icebreaker activity where the Task Force Members 
discussed tradeoffs and strategic priorities.  
 
5.4 
Guest Lecture and Discussion - Inclusive Economics 
Jordan Manning, Manager, Economic Advisory, Deloitte, delivered a guest lecture 
on inclusive economic growth, highlighting the economic benefits of diversity and 
inclusion, and engaged the Task Force members in a about strategies for 
attracting and retaining talent, supporting underrepresented groups, and 
leveraging Burnaby's diversity. 

Mayor’s Task Force on Burnaby Economic Growth and Resilience Meeting    
Minutes March 5, 2026 
 
 
4 
*Dr. Vidya Vankayala arrived at 6:17 pm 
5.5 
Interactive Discussion and Group Activities 
A. Fung, Facilitator, led a series of interactive discussions focused on indigenous 
communities, visible minorities, newcomers and identifying strategic priorities.  
*Mayor Hurley left the meeting at 7:14 pm and did not return 
 
5.6 
Closing Remarks 
In closing, A. Fung, Facilitator, advised that Deloitte will conduct stakeholder 
interviews with sectors not represented on the Task Force and deploy a survey 
on behalf of the City of Burnaby, in partnership with the Burnaby Board of Trade, 
to validate the top strategic priorities of the Task Force.  
 
The next workshop will be held on April 16, 2026 at 5:00 pm. 
 
6. 
OTHER BUSINESS 
There was no other business brought before the Task Force at this time. 
 
7. 
ADJOURNMENT 
By unanimous consent and without objection, the Task Force meeting adjourned at 8:10 
pm. 
 
 
 
 
 
Councillor James Wang 
VICE CHAIR 
 
Denise Chak 
ADMINISTRATIVE OFFICER

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Burnaby Mayor's Task Force Moves from Strategy to Action on Economic Growth
briefs.summary: Burnaby's Mayor's Task Force on Economic Growth and Resilience is set to hold its sixth workshop on April 16, 2026, where members will review strategic priorities and move from planning to action. The Task Force — made up of local business leaders, city councillors, and community representatives — has been working with consultants to shape Burnaby's long-term economic strategy, with a focus on inclusive growth for diverse communities and small businesses.
briefs.action_required: Attend the April 16 workshop at Christine Sinclair Community Centre if you want to follow or influence Burnaby's economic strategy.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Mayor's Task Force Workshop #6 — Strategy to Action session

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Burnaby Task Force Explores Inclusive Economic Growth Strategy
briefs.summary: Burnaby's Mayor's Task Force on Economic Growth and Resilience met on March 5, 2026 to discuss 'inclusive economics' — the idea that city growth should create opportunity for everyone, including newcomers, Indigenous communities, and visible minorities. The Task Force heard from a Deloitte expert and is working toward a set of strategic priorities. A survey and stakeholder interviews are planned to help shape those priorities before the next workshop on April 16, 2026.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the survey from Burnaby Board of Trade — it's your chance to shape the city's economic strategy.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Mayor's Task Force on Economic Growth — next workshop

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Burnaby Mayor's Task Force on Economic Growth Moves to Strategy Phase
briefs.summary: Burnaby's Mayor's Task Force on Economic Growth and Resilience is set to hold its sixth workshop on April 16, 2026, shifting from research to action by reviewing strategic priorities and engagement findings. The Task Force — made up of local business leaders, city councillors, and community representatives — is working to shape Burnaby's economic development strategy, with a focus on inclusive growth that benefits a wide range of businesses and residents.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the Task Force's final strategy recommendations — they could shape city programs and support for small businesses.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Mayor's Task Force Workshop 6 — Strategy to Action session

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Burnaby Mayor's Task Force Reviews Economic Growth Strategy
briefs.summary: Burnaby's Mayor's Task Force on Economic Growth and Resilience is set to meet on April 16, 2026 to review strategic priorities and discuss findings from community engagement. The Task Force — made up of local business leaders, city councillors, and community representatives — is working to shape a city-wide economic strategy focused on inclusive growth, support for small businesses, and long-term resilience.
briefs.action_required: Attend or follow the April 16 Task Force meeting to stay informed on Burnaby's emerging economic strategy.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Mayor's Task Force Workshop #6 — Strategy to Action meeting date

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Burnaby Mayor's Task Force on Economic Growth Moves to Action Planning
briefs.summary: Burnaby's Mayor's Task Force on Economic Growth and Resilience is set to hold its sixth workshop on April 16, 2026. The session will focus on turning strategic priorities into action, reviewing engagement findings, and holding group discussions — building toward a formal economic strategy for the city.
briefs.action_required: Attend or follow the April 16 workshop to stay informed on Burnaby's emerging economic strategy.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Mayor's Task Force Workshop #6 — Strategy to Action session
